Extraordinary earnings growth

Focus on value and diversified U.S. stocks

commentators.name: Jurrien Timmer
commentators.key_quote: Artificial intelligence remains one of the defining forces behind today's market advance, but the real test is whether earnings can keep pace with expectations.
key_views: AI is a defining force, but earnings growth is key
Diversification is important
Interest rate risk exists, but earnings growth can offset it

commentators.name: Savita Subramanian
commentators.key_quote: Aﬅer a blowout 1Q (S&P 500 EPS +27% YoY), consensus expects S&P 500 EPS to grow 22% YoY in 2Q. Expectations are elevated, but we see no signs of earnings momentum rolling over.
key_views: Elevated earnings expectations
Recommends cyclical companies
Most bearish S&P 500 outlook for 2026 (7,100)

commentators.name: Lisa Shalett
commentators.key_quote: 2026 should deliver roughly 10% potential upside for the S&P 500 based on the embedded earnings strength, but we are NOT as bullish as others at MS & Co.
key_views: Cautious about pure passive index trade and small-cap beta
Selectivity will matter
Focus on large-cap quality equities

commentators.name: Marko Kolanovic
commentators.key_quote: The S&P 500 is expected to fall as stocks face historically high levels of concentration.
key_views: Stocks face historically high concentration
Imbalance between large and small stocks is unsustainable
Potential for cyclical downturn to 'purge' valuations
commentators.price_target_or_view: S&P 500 could fall to the 5,000s or lower

commentators.name: Lance Roberts
commentators.key_quote: Markets remain locked in an increasingly tight consolidation pattern, with price compression signaling that a decisive move may be approaching.
key_views: Momentum trade weakening
Rotation towards value stocks and broader market participation
Geopolitical tensions add uncertainty

equity.synthesis: Commentators are divided on the near-term outlook for the S&P 500, with some forecasting continued gains driven by AI and strong earnings, while others warn of stretched valuations and potential pullbacks. Geopolitical tensions and oil prices remain key concerns across the spectrum.

commentators.name: Mike Novogratz
commentators.key_quote: I think 60 is going to hold, and I think 80 is going to be a top. And if we can get through 80, then 100 is going to be a top.
key_views: Bitcoin likely to trade between $60k-$80k unless catalysts emerge.
CLARITY Act passage, Fed rate cuts, and renewed buyer base needed for $100k.
Crypto has lost its speculative 'vibe' but infrastructure is still developing.
commentators.btc_view: Cautiously Bullish, range-bound between $60k-$80k in the near term.

commentators.name: Michael Saylor
commentators.key_quote: The impulse to change Bitcoin's rules merely to prevent others from using Bitcoin in ways you disapprove of is statist and alien to a community rooted in liberty.
key_views: Strongly opposes BIP-110, viewing it as a threat to Bitcoin's core principles.
Advocates for Bitcoin's base layer to remain conservative and permissionless.
Believes Bitcoin has won the debate over digital capital.
commentators.btc_view: Very Bullish, sees Bitcoin as the premier digital capital.
commentators.price_target: Implied long-term bullishness, sees capital flows dictating growth.

commentators.name: Cathie Wood
commentators.key_quote: Bitcoin is currently bottoming out, testing a massive base, and preparing for an unstoppable run in the second half of 2026.
key_views: Believes Bitcoin has entered a bottoming process and will resume its uptrend.
AI's boom creates an 'Age of Abundance' that highlights Bitcoin's scarcity.
Institutional adoption is a key driver for Bitcoin's future growth.
commentators.btc_view: Very Bullish, expects a strong run in the second half of 2026.
commentators._validation_warning: Stance auto-corrected: target 2,030 implies -96.9% downside from current ~65,525, conflicts with 'Very Bullish' stance.

commentators.name: Tom Lee
commentators.key_quote: Bitcoin could eventually reach $2 million per coin, a figure derived from comparing Bitcoin's current market capitalization to that of gold.
key_views: Predicts Bitcoin could reach $2 million and Ethereum $250,000.
Believes the four-year halving cycle is breaking down, with ETF demand being dominant.
Sees Bitcoin's long-term story intact despite recent pullbacks.
commentators.btc_view: Very Bullish, with a $2 million price target.
commentators.eth_view: Very Bullish, with a $250,000 price target.

commentators.name: PlanB
commentators.key_quote: Based on stock-to-flow valuation, BTC could still go to $500k ($250k-$1m range) … this cycle (2026-2028).
key_views: Bitcoin is undervalued and may still fall below realized price.
The current cycle's peak might occur between 2026-2028, termed 'max pain'.
Stock-to-Flow model still indicates a significantly higher valuation.
commentators.btc_view: Bullish, with potential for significant upside.

commentators.name: Lyn Alden
commentators.key_quote: Personally, this is the lowest bitcoin investment sentiment I've seen.
key_views: Bitcoin sentiment is at its lowest point in the current cycle.
Long-term success depends on Bitcoin's own merits, not external catalysts.
Cautious on price outlook, may not set new all-time high this year.
commentators.btc_view: Neutral to Cautiously Bearish on short-term price, but sees long-term merit.
commentators.price_target: May not set new all-time high this year.

commentators.name: Anthony Pompliano
commentators.key_quote: Bitcoin will go back up when there are more buyers than sellers.
key_views: Bitcoin's current slump is just volatility, and it will eventually rise.
Views Bitcoin as a hedge against dollar devaluation.
Will host a debate with Peter Schiff on Bitcoin being a bubble.
commentators.btc_view: Very Bullish, expects recovery and long-term growth.

commentators.name: Raoul Pal
commentators.key_quote: The world is entering the largest capital investment cycle in human history—and that this could prevent a traditional Bitcoin and crypto bear market.
key_views: The crypto market could grow to $100 trillion by 2034.
AI, autonomous agents, and on-chain settlement are key drivers.
Views Bitcoin as humanity's pension plan.
commentators.btc_view: Very Bullish, sees massive long-term growth potential.
commentators.eth_view: Very Bullish, sees massive long-term growth potential.

commentators.name: Vitalik Buterin
commentators.key_quote: Lean Ethereum, a rebuild touching nearly every layer of the protocol, will take three to four years and rival the Merge in scope.
key_views: Ethereum's next major upgrade ('Lean Ethereum') will take 3-4 years.
AI is surpassing humans in a broad range of capabilities.
Proposed a new programming language to make AI proofs readable.
commentators.eth_view: Focused on technical development and long-term upgrades.

commentators.name: Nic Carter
commentators.key_quote: Bitcoin's quantum-resistance lag may become Ethereum's bull case.
key_views: Urges Bitcoin developers to implement quantum-resistant cryptography.
Warns of a potential 'corporate takeover' if developers don't act swiftly.
Views Ethereum as having a more advanced post-quantum roadmap.
commentators.btc_view: Concerned about Bitcoin's quantum resistance preparedness.
commentators.eth_view: Views Ethereum as having an advantage in quantum resistance.

crypto.synthesis: This week's crypto commentary shows a divided market. While many remain bullish on Bitcoin's long-term prospects, citing institutional adoption and technological advancements, others express caution due to current market sentiment and regulatory uncertainties. Ethereum's development roadmap and AI's impact on the crypto space are also key discussion points.
